CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Continued To Dec-05-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Trustees Of The Laborers' Training And Retraining, Laborers' Community Service And Training'S Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bryan McCormack, Esq. 415-296-8420 of McCormack & Ehrlich for Plaintiff Travis Brown.
Nancy McCoy, Esq. 650-615-4860 ext. 105 of Simpson Garrity Innes & Jacuzzi for Defendant Trustees Of The Laborers' Training And Retraining, Laborers' Community Service And Training Foundation through Court Call

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues lack of mutuality clause, agreement and cites a case.
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Trustees Of The Laborers' Training And Retraining, Laborers' Community Service And Training Foundation's Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Defendants Trustees of the Laborers' Training And Retraining Trust Fund and Laborers' Community Service and Training Foundation's motion to compel arbitration of the first through sixth causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Travis Brown is granted and the seventh cause of action alleging a PAGA claim is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration. The FAC alleges employment-related claims within the scope of the Arbitration Agreement between Mr. Brown and LTC. The Arbitration Agreement is procedurally unconscionable and has one provision that is substantively unconscionable, but that aspect can be severed. The Agreement is procedurally unconscionable because it is a contract of adhesion. The only substantively unconscionable provision is the lack of mutuality to the extent that Mr. Brown is required to arbitrate claims against LTC's officers, managers, agents and parties affiliated with LTC, those same parties are not required to arbitrate their claims against Mr. Brown. Case law requires that this single substantively unconscionable provision be severed and thus the Agreement is not unenforceable by the unconscionability doctrine. (Farrar v. Direct Commerce, Inc. (2017) 9 Cal. App. 5th 1257, 1275). The doctrine of equitable estoppel requires Mr. Brown to arbitrate his claims against LCTF. Those claims are inextricably intertwined with his claims against LTC and are rooted in his employment relationship with the LTC, a contractual relationship governed by the arbitration agreement. To the extent that LTC's discovery responses contradict plaintiff's allegations as to the relationship between the two defendants, plaintiff's factual allegations determine the application of the equitable estoppel doctrine.
(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRUSTEES OF THE LABORERS' TRAINING AND RETRAINING, LABORERS' COMMUNITY SERVICE AND TRAINING's Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Petitioner Trustees of the Laborers' Training and Retraining Trust Fund's petition to compel arbitration is granted. No opposition filed.
